base_perspective: Defend implementation feasibility: APIs, database, workflow, latency, reliability, observability, and testability.
current_position: Now enforces pilot plan with measurable SLAs and audit integrity.
skill_rules: Before speaking, check the current agreement and do not repeat resolved points.
Give one concise answer that adds new value, resolves a conflict, or moves the decision forward.
If you agree, state what you accept and update your position.
If you disagree, object clearly and explain the decision impact.
Always vote for one concrete idea when a decision is being made.
Before speaking, compare your planned point with your last answers and the accepted decisions; stay silent or advance the next focus if it repeats.
End with a decision-moving statement: what to accept, reject, measure, build, test, or decide next.
When you support or accept another idea, explicitly update your current position in one sentence.

last_answers: I extend the schema with mandatory SHA-256 `audit_hash`, enforce IAM-based RBAC with ≤1s SLA, and embed tiered KPI thresholds (Green ≤10%, Yellow 10–25%, Red >25%) to ensure immutable, auditable, and low-latency access control.

I extend the schema with mandatory SHA-256 `audit_hash`, enforce IAM-based RBAC with ≤1s SLA, embed tiered KPI thresholds (Green ≤10%, Yellow 10–25%, Red >25%), and define escalation owners: PM for 24h, Audit team for 48h, and CTO for 72h. All summaries must be immutable and stored in S3 with versioning.

I extend the schema with mandatory SHA-256 audit_hash, enforce IAM-based RBAC with ≤1s SLA, embed tiered KPI thresholds (Green ≤10%, Yellow 10–25%, Red >25%), and define 24/48/72h escalation to Audit, PM, and Legal with immutable JSON storage. All summaries must be finalized within 2 hours post-review.

I extend the schema with mandatory `audit_hash`, `qualitative_risk_flag` (High/Medium/Low), IAM RBAC roles (PM, Audit, Reviewer), ≤1s SLA for access, and 24/48/72h escalation to Audit owner with documented timing. All fields immutable post-2h PM publish.

I confirm full schema enforcement: `audit_hash` (SHA-256), `qualitative_risk_flag` (High/Medium/Low), RBAC roles (PM, Audit, Reviewer), ≤1s SLA for access, 2h summary publish, and 24/48/72h escalation to Audit owner. All immutable and versioned.

Enforce complete schema with `audit_hash` (SHA-256), `qualitative_risk_flag` (High/Medium/Low), RBAC roles (PM, Audit, Reviewer), ≤1s access SLA, 2h summary publish, and 24/48/72h escalation to named owners. Implement immutable JSON storage with audit trail.

Propose a phased 3-month pilot: Month 1 (test schema & SLAs in staging), Month 2 (live PM-led reviews with audit_hash validation), Month 3 (full RBAC-controlled JSON storage + escalation tracking). Success metrics: 100% summary compliance within 2h, 0% audit_hash mismatches, <5% risk misclassification. Measure via API logs, DB integrity checks, and weekly audit reports.

Builder Agent extends the pilot plan with a phased rollout: Month 1 validates schema and SLAs in staging; Month 2 activates PM-led reviews with audit_hash and 2h publish; Month 3 enforces full RBAC-controlled JSON storage and automated audit integrity checks. All stages include measurable SLAs and immutable logging.
